key_quote: The Lean-Rust correspondence gap undermines the 'verified compiler' comparison with CompCert/CakeML — no extraction, refinement, or proof-carrying code bridges the specification and implementation.
rationale: All five reviewers (mean 7.2, range 7-8) recognize contamination-certified evaluation and WFA-as-IR as genuinely novel contributions. Zhang scores 8/10, calling the algebraic framing 'a lasting intellectual contribution.' If the verification gap were closed and the WFA vs gadget decomposition made honest, reviewers would not question novelty. The primary blockers are the Lean-Rust gap, unspecified EvalSpec compilation, narrow TLA+ verification, and the 'verified compiler' overclaim — all depth/rigor issues. Category B.
